relatedness_reasoning: Both files concern customer account financial status; inbound provides account balance data from accounts department to credit department, and outbound delivers arrears assessment from credit department to customer services. The credit department consumes account balances and produces arrears status, forming a logical pipeline. However, outbound fields like arrearsMonths, riskLevel, and actionRequired require computation/assessment not directly present in inbound, indicating transformation rather than simple pass-through.

mappings.reasoning: Direct correspondence between message identifiers; naming convention differs (camelCase vs snake_case).
Both are ISO8601 timestamps marking message generation; inbound sent_at repurposed as outbound timestamp.
Outbound sourceSystem is a constant enum value (CREDIT_DEPARTMENT); not derived from inbound data.
Outbound destinationSystem is a constant enum value (CUSTOMER_SERVICES); not derived from inbound data.
Direct field correspondence; naming convention differs (camelCase vs snake_case).
Inbound account_id maps to outbound accountNumber; both uniquely identify accounts.

Inbound account_type enum (checking, savings, credit_card) must be mapped to outbound productType enum (MORTGAGE, CREDIT_CARD, PERSONAL_LOAN); only credit_card overlaps directly, others require business logic.
Inbound balance (which can be negative for credit cards indicating amount owed) is transformed to arrears amount; requires filtering/calculation to isolate only arrears portion.
Direct field copy; both are ISO 4217 currency codes with identical enum constraint (GBP).
No inbound field provides month count; requires external calculation or lookup based on arrears history not present in inbound schema.
Arrears status classification (ACTIVE_ARREARS, EARLY_ARREARS, NO_ARREARS) requires business logic and historical data not available in inbound account balance snapshot.
Arrears start date requires historical tracking; inbound only provides current balance and last_updated timestamp, not arrears commencement date.
Last payment date is not present in inbound account balance data; requires transaction history not provided.
Next payment due date requires account terms/schedule data not present in inbound balance snapshot.
Risk assessment (HIGH, MEDIUM, LOW) requires credit scoring logic and external data not derivable from inbound balance alone.
Action flag requires business rule evaluation and policy decisions not present in inbound data.
Free-text notes are contextual and not derivable from structured inbound balance data.
